content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" (fetched from https://runtracker.example/privacy), but App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-16 imports CoreLocation, requests when-in-use authorization, and stores locations in `route`; the description.txt also advertises GPS run mapping.
Pierre: Apple checks that the privacy policy honestly reflects actual data practices, and a direct contradiction like this is a classic 5.1.1 rejection. The code clearly processes and stores location (route array of CLLocation) and the metadata even promotes GPS tracking, yet the policy claims the app works "entirely without knowing where you are." Update the privacy policy to disclose location collection/processing (even if on-device only) before submission.
